summary: Closed ConfigError smoke coverage to 5/5 emit sites. Added test_consumer_supervisor_fails_with_friendly_message_on_corrupt_git_repo: bootstraps a consumer, initializes git + commits the bootstrap, seeds the example queue, then writes 'garbage-not-a-valid-ref\n' into .git/HEAD so the subsequent `git diff --name-only --relative` call exits 129 with 'Not a git repository' warning while the .git/ directory still exists. The supervisor's policy.git_dirty_paths catches the resulting CalledProcessError, hits the else-branch (because .git exists), and raises ConfigError with 'git command failed in <repo_root>: git diff --name-only --relative\nstderr: ...'. The test asserts no Traceback substring, the 'git command failed in' phrase, and the consumer's path. The supervisor smoke class now has 9 tests + 2 sync-resilience tests added in the prior slice (11 in the smoke class total); the full automation-check has 122.
validations_passed: manual probe: garbage-in-.git/HEAD produces exit code 129 from `git diff --name-only --relative` while .git/ persists, so the else-branch of policy.git_dirty_paths fires

configerror_emit_sites_now_smoke_tested.site: git_dirty_paths CalledProcessError with .git present (generic git failure)

scope_observed_from: git diff --name-only --relative behavior under garbage HEAD: exits 129 with 'Not a git repository' warning, .git/ persists
behavior_proven: When .git exists but git diff fails (corrupt HEAD), the supervisor takes the else-branch of git_dirty_paths and reports 'git command failed in <path>' rather than the 'not a Git repository' message.
The supervisor's _cli_entry catches the ConfigError and prints stop: prefix to stderr; no Python traceback reaches the consumer.
The corruption pattern (garbage in .git/HEAD) is deterministic across git versions; both Apple-shipped and Homebrew git produce the same exit-129 behavior.
gaps_named_for_follow_up: Workflow templates in manifest (promotes external CI scaffold into Owlory as reusable templates).
Real third-party repo migration when a target is named explicitly by the user.
Sync tool --force-templates flag for consumers who want to re-baseline template files after the first-time-only semantic landed.
not_claimed: Every git failure mode is covered. The smoke test exercises one realistic corruption pattern (bad HEAD); other corruptions (missing .git/objects, locked .git/index, fsck failures) take the same code path so are presumed safe but not directly asserted.

policy.git_dirty_paths handles all subprocess error types gracefully. FileNotFoundError and CalledProcessError are caught; OSError variants (permission denied) are not exercised. Their behavior would propagate unmodified — that's a defensible boundary for a smoke proof.
Real consumer machines have experienced corrupt repos and seen the friendly message. The smoke test bootstraps temp consumers only.
residual_risks: The test asserts the substring 'git command failed in' verbatim. Changing the policy.ConfigError message text requires updating this assertion in lockstep with the other four ConfigError smoke tests.

Future git versions could change the exit code or stderr for corrupt HEAD; the test would still detect ConfigError emission, but the assertion on the consumer path remains stable because policy.git_dirty_paths injects repo_root into the message regardless of git's stderr.
recommended_next_reason: Queue is empty. ConfigError smoke coverage is now 5/5. Remaining named boundaries: workflow templates in manifest, real third-party migration, --force-templates flag.
